title: Combination of COUNT and NOT leads to incorrect results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255), count_value INT DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, count_value) VALUES (1, 'A', 2), (2, 'B', 1), (3, 'C', NULL);
SELECT COUNT(*) > 0 AS res FROM (SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM test_table GROUP BY id) AS i WHERE NOT (i.cnt IS NULL OR i.cnt > 1);
SELECT COUNT(*) > 0 AS res FROM (SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM test_table GROUP BY id) AS i WHERE NOT (i.cnt < 2);
SELECT COUNT(*) = 0 AS res FROM (SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM test_table GROUP BY id) AS i WHERE 1 <= i.cnt;
key_bug_features: COUNT and NOT combination yields incorrect query results
